title: Bottom tab bar: fix small tap targets + lean into Liquid Glass
description:
User report: the bottom navigation bar is not very responsive — you seemingly have to tap almost exactly on the glyph for the tab to switch, and roughly half of taps feel like they do nothing. Frustrating. User also wants to "lean into" Liquid Glass for the tab bar to make it look nicer, and pointed at the Axiom skill set for guidance.

Where: Sources/DesignSystem/LiquidGlassTabBar.swift (the custom bottom tab bar; EnoughTab cases Learn/Reviews/Progress/Browse). Rendered in Sources/App/MainShellView.swift. Related design system: Sources/DesignSystem/EnoughTab.swift (tab symbols/titles/axIDs).

Likely root cause of the dead taps (for conversion to confirm, not resolve here): in LiquidGlassTabBar.swift each tab is a Button (lines ~10-27) whose label is a VStack(image + text) with `.frame(maxWidth: .infinity)` and `.frame(minHeight: 58)`, styled `.buttonStyle(PlainButtonStyle())`. There is NO `.contentShape(Rectangle())` on the label. With a plain button and no explicit content shape, SwiftUI hit-tests only the non-transparent content (the ~18pt glyph + 10pt text), NOT the empty expanded frame around it — so the large-looking tab cell is mostly non-tappable and only a direct hit on the glyph/label registers. Adding `.contentShape(Rectangle())` to the label (so the whole maxWidth/minHeight frame is the hit area) is the classic fix; worth also sanity-checking the outer capsule padding and any overlay that could be intercepting touches. This is a hit-testing bug, not a gesture-conflict, most likely.

Liquid Glass state today: the bar ALREADY applies `.glassEffect(.regular, in: Capsule())` (line 37) plus a soft shadow — so it is partially on Liquid Glass already. "Lean into it" is an enhancement, not a from-scratch adoption: e.g. interactive glass (`.glassEffect(.regular.interactive())`), wrapping the tab items in a GlassEffectContainer, tinting the glass on the selected tab with the accentTheme, and/or a glass morph/selection animation. This is iOS 26 Liquid Glass (SwiftUI `glassEffect`), current deployment target of the app.

Axiom guidance the user flagged: relevant Axiom skills are axiom:axiom-swiftui (iOS 26 SwiftUI incl. Liquid Glass), axiom:axiom-design (HIG + Liquid Glass patterns), and the axiom:liquid-glass-auditor agent (scans for Liquid Glass adoption/migration opportunities) — conversion should consult these before shaping the visual-enhancement portion.

Scoping note for conversion (defer the decision): this is arguably TWO tasks — (1) a bugfix for the tap targets (small, high-value, unblocks usability — likely `contentShape` on the label), and (2) a visual polish/enhancement leaning further into Liquid Glass (subjective/taste — may warrant a needs-human review or a build-behind-a-toggle + pick-a-winner chain). Both touch the same file (LiquidGlassTabBar.swift), possibly EnoughTab.swift.

title: Debug: reset app back through onboarding without relaunch
description:
User wants a debug setting to "flash the app to go back through the initial flows and strip everything down" — i.e. wipe all state and re-enter onboarding from scratch, to make testing on device a lot easier.

IMPORTANT existing state (this is largely an ENHANCEMENT, not a from-scratch build — conversion should confirm, not assume): the app ALREADY has a debug reset. Sources/Features/Debug/DebugReset.swift defines a "Danger zone" DebugSection with a "Reset all data" row (id "reset-all", subtitle "Wipes trip, purchases, SRS progress and history") whose action calls AppReset.wipeAll(context:userDefaults:) and returns the message "Data wiped — relaunch the app". So the wipe exists — the friction is that it tells you to MANUALLY relaunch, which on a physical device means killing and reopening the app. The user almost certainly wants the reset to ALSO immediately route back to the onboarding flow (no manual relaunch), effectively "flashing" the app to its first-run state in place.

Relevant anchors:
- Sources/Features/Debug/DebugReset.swift (existing reset action).
- AppReset.wipeAll(...) — the actual wipe (grep for its definition; likely Sources/App/ or a Persistence/ file).
- Sources/App/RootView.swift — decides onboarding-vs-main shell; this is likely where an in-place reset must re-trigger onboarding (reset whatever "has onboarded" flag / AppState routing it keys off, so the wipe drops you back into OnboardingFlowView without relaunch).
- Sources/App/AppState.swift — app-level routing/state.
- Sources/Features/Onboarding/OnboardingFlowView.swift (+ WelcomeView, CountryPickerView, TripShapeView, PlanView, OnboardingCompleter, OnboardingDraft) — the initial flow to return to.
- The debug menu framework already exists: Sources/Features/Debug/DebugRegistry.swift, DebugUnlock.swift (how the hidden debug menu is unlocked/entered — related to prior task T037 "Debug menu shell: hidden entry via shake / launch argument", which was failed/superseded — worth checking current entry method), plus DebugTimeTravel.swift, DebugNotifications.swift, DebugAbout.swift. Related debug tasks: T040 (force learned cards due), T102 (debug action to make studied cards due now).
- Sources/App/LaunchArguments.swift already handles a -demo-state launch flag (seeds demo data via DemoSeeder). A reset-and-onboard could plausibly reuse similar plumbing.

Why it matters: on-device testing currently requires manually relaunching after a wipe (and the wipe may not even re-show onboarding without a fresh launch); a one-tap "reset to first-run and drop me into onboarding" makes iterating on the onboarding + initial flows dramatically faster.

Deferred to conversion (do NOT decide here): whether this is a NEW debug row or an enhancement of the existing "Reset all data" row; whether it must avoid a relaunch entirely (reset RootView/AppState routing in place) or is acceptable to require one; exactly which onboarding entry point to return to; and the acceptance bar. Check first whether AppReset.wipeAll already clears the onboarding flag — if it does, the gap may be purely the in-place re-route rather than the wipe.
